articles.title: Nothing’s noise-canceling CMF Buds 2A are down to $19.99 for the rest of today
articles.description: It’s not every day you find a decent pair of wireless earbuds with active noise cancellation, a transparency mode, and app support for less than $20, which is why the current lighting deal on the CMF Buds 2A stands out. Now through 11:15PM ET today, April 7th, Nothing’s budget earbuds are available on Amazon in […]
articles.author: Sheena Vasani

articles.title: Amazon is ending support for older Kindles and Kindle Fires
articles.description: Amazon has announced that starting on May 20th, 2026, Kindle e-readers and Kindle Fire devices released in 2012 and earlier will "no longer be able to purchase, borrow, or download new content via the Kindle Store," Amazon spokesperson Jackie Burke wrote in an email to The Verge. Users will still be able to read books […]
articles.author: Andrew Liszewski

articles.title: Spotify's Prompted Playlists can help you find new podcasts to listen to
articles.description: On Tuesday, Spotify expanded its Prompted Playlists feature to include podcasts, an update that could make it easier for Premium users to find new shows to listen to. Prompted Playlists were originally launched as a beta feature in December, but previously only worked for music. You can use the feature to effectively generate customized Discover […]
articles.author: Stevie Bonifield

articles.title: You can now turn 2D apps into 3D while using the Galaxy XR headset
articles.description: Android XR is getting a new feature that turns 2D apps, websites, images, and videos into "3D experiences." The feature, which Google calls "auto-spatialization," was initially announced last year, and it's launching on Tuesday as an experimental feature for Samsung Galaxy XR headsets. Here's a video from Google that gives you an idea about how […]
articles.author: Jay Peters

articles.title: I wish this selfie phone case was better for selfies
articles.description: The Selfix phone case is a classic example of a great idea in theory that falls apart in practice. It's a case for the iPhone 17 Pro with a circular screen on the back. It mirrors the middle of your main phone screen, making it easier to frame up selfies using your phone's higher-quality rear […]
articles.author: Allison Johnson

articles.title: Samsung’s Galaxy S26 Ultra is $200 off for the first time
articles.description: My colleague Allison Johnson loved the Privacy Display in her review of the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra. The display feature, which blocks onlookers from getting a peep at what’s on the screen when viewed from the side, is worth getting the S26 Ultra for if you are serious about privacy during your commute or elsewhere. […]
articles.author: Cameron Faulkner

articles.title: A new Anthropic model found security problems 'in every major operating system and web browser'
articles.description: Anthropic is debuting a new AI model as part of a cybersecurity partnership with Nvidia, Google, Amazon Web Services, Apple, Microsoft, and other companies. Project Glasswing, as it's called, is billed as a way for large companies, and potentially even the government, to flag vulnerabilities in their systems with virtually no human intervention. Anthropic is […]
articles.author: Hayden Field

articles.title: The rugged Bose Soundlink Flex is 25 percent off right now
articles.description: We saw a host of excellent Bose discounts during Amazon’s so-called Big Spring Sale last month; however, none of them were quite as steep as the current price drop we’re seeing on the latest Bose Soundlink Flex. Right now, the portable speaker is on sale at Amazon, Walmart, and Best Buy for $119 ($40 off), […]
articles.author: Brandon Widder

articles.title: Vertical browser tabs are better and you should use them
articles.description: Google's Chrome browser is getting a couple of new features, both of them extremely welcome and wildly overdue. The first is a reading mode, which does what it already does in most other browsers: strip out a lot of website cruft to make pages easier to read. Reading mode is good, you should use it, […]
articles.author: David Pierce

articles.title: Suno and major music labels reportedly clash over AI music sharing
articles.description: The AI-powered musicmaker Suno is struggling to reach licensing deals with Universal Music Group and Sony Music Entertainment. That's according to a report from the Financial Times, which says both sides can't agree on whether users should be able to share the AI-generated songs they create. "Universal wants AI-generated tracks to stay inside apps such […]
articles.author: Emma Roth
